scanner_blind_spots.reason: CEO departure triggered 73% algorithmic selloff from $175 to $47; the scanner's fundamentals-based rules cannot distinguish leadership disruption from business deterioration. Insider cluster ($1.5M) at the bottom and intact $4.2B FCF/60% gross margins confirm this is event-driven mispricing — exactly what algorithmic scanners miss.

Spin-off announcement (NBCU+Sky, June 29) creates pure-play broadband value at 4.7x PE. The scanner looks at Comcast as struggling cable and passes; the discovery process identified the structural catalyst that re-rates the surviving entity 41% above current levels.

Ten board directors buying on the same day is the largest blue-chip director cluster of 2026. The scanner doesn't read Form 4 filings in context — it can't distinguish one executive's routine purchase from ten directors coordinating at a multi-year low. Insider signal detection at this scale requires human pattern recognition.

The scanner doesn't classify utilities as AI infrastructure plays. Vistra's Meta+AWS nuclear/gas PPAs (6,400MW combined — the largest AI power deals in history) represent the AI energy scarcity trade. Discovery identified this cross-sector connection; sector-classified scanners cannot.

catalysts_general: Tier 3/4: Q1 FY2026 EPS $2.21 vs $1.77 estimate (blowout); management cautious tone drove selloff; Wolfe downgraded; tariff risk partially de-risked; analyst target $260

Discovery: Classic 'sell the news on good news' panic: stock dropped 16.2% on June 4 Q1 2026 earnings despite beating EPS by 32% ($2.22 vs $1.68 estimate) with revenue of $1.29B beating estimates, and management RAISED full-year guidance to $5.40–5.48B revenue and $480–502M net income — the sell-off was driven by cautious consumer-tone commentary, not fundamentals; with 25 analyst Buy consensus and $261 avg price target (38.6% upside from $188), Q2 earnings in August represent the re-rating catalyst when the consumer narrative normalizes. Bear case: consumer discretionary headwinds are real and lower-income shoppers may trade down further.
value_picks.analyst_take: Q1 blowout beat ($2.21 EPS vs $1.77 est); sold off 10%+ on cautious tone; tariff risk partially de-risked; analyst target $260; defensive retailer in consumer-stressed env

catalysts_general: Tier 3/4: China added MP to export control list June 22; DoD $400M equity stake; insider NET selling YTD despite positive narrative; DCF fair value ~$22 vs current $53

Discovery: On June 22, 2026 China added MP Materials directly to its export control list alongside 9 other US firms — a diplomatic escalation that paradoxically strengthens MP's commercial case as the sole large-scale rare earth miner in the Western Hemisphere; with DoD's $400M equity investment providing floor and downstream magnet manufacturing (Independence facility) beginning H2 2026, MP is transitioning from concentrate to high-value magnets just as China tightens its chokehold on processing.
growth_picks.analyst_take: China export ban confirms strategic moat; DoD $400M equity stake; but insider NET selling and DCF fair value ~$22 caution against current $53
